title: Koch → Atlas Network → Argentine Think Tanks → Milei: The International Libertarian Funding Pipeline
summary: The Koch brothers and allied US donors fund Atlas Network, which channels money to ~12 Argentine think tanks that shaped Milei's ideology, trained his cadre, and provided policy blueprints for his government. Between 2010 and 2021, Atlas distributed approximately $12 million USD to Latin American think tanks (IRS 990 filings analyzed by DeSmog). Argentina's Ley 26.215 prohibits foreign contributions to political parties, but the pipeline bypasses this by funding NGOs/think tanks that do 'economic education' rather than direct party financing.

stage_1_donors.description: US billionaires, foundations, and dark money vehicles that fund Atlas Network

entities.name: Charles Koch Foundation
entities.note: Koch brothers were key to installing Alejandro Chafuen as Atlas CEO in 1991

entities.name: Donors Trust / Donors Capital Fund
entities.role: Dark money ATM — anonymizes donor identities before passing funds to Atlas and affiliates
entities.note: Founded 1999 by Whitney Ball, an 'ardent libertarian' who raised funds for Koch-founded Cato Institute. Alejandro Chafuen was a founding member.

entities.name: ExxonMobil
entities.role: Corporate funder — checks of $15,000-$50,000 at a time sent to Atlas
entities.note: In 1999, ExxonMobil-funded programs included ~24 free-market think tanks including Argentina's Fundacion Libertad

stage_2_atlas_network.description: US-based nonprofit that channels funds to 500+ think tanks worldwide
stage_2_atlas_network.name: Atlas Network (formerly Atlas Economic Research Foundation)
stage_2_atlas_network.annual_disbursement: $5,000,000+ (2016 figure; $10M+ annually via grants and prizes)

key_figure.name: Alejandro Chafuen
key_figure.significance: First Atlas Networker in Latin America (1991). Founding member of Donors Trust. Later moved to Acton Institute. Regular visitor to Casa Rosada.

umbrella_networks.RELIAL: Red Liberal para America Latina — connects dozens of organizations in Brazil, Chile, Colombia, Mexico with direct links to right-wing parties
umbrella_networks.HACER: Hispanic American Center for Economic Research — linked to RELIAL and Liberal International

stage_3_argentine_think_tanks.description: ~12 Argentine organizations affiliated with Atlas Network that receive funding, training, and networking

organizations.name: Fundacion Libertad
organizations.role: Most influential Atlas node in Argentina. Seedbed of neoliberal ideas and training space for right-wing politicians and economists.
organizations.atlas_relationship: Direct partner, receives economic support plus corporate sponsorship (Vicentin, Paladini, Nova)
organizations.exxon_connection: Included in 1999 ExxonMobil-funded program of ~24 think tanks
organizations.political_impact: Key role promoting Macri and Milei candidacies

organizations.name: Fundacion Atlas 1853
organizations.role: Major think tank. Diana Mondino (Milei's Foreign Minister) was on its Academic Council.
organizations.atlas_relationship: Direct partner, one of two flagship Atlas organizations in Argentina

organizations.name: Libertad y Progreso
organizations.role: Economic policy think tank. Contributed to Milei's deregulation and spending-cut program.
organizations.atlas_relationship: Direct partner, received January 2024 funding post-Milei inauguration to promote reform agenda with Cato Institute
organizations.mont_pelerin_connection: Members Eduardo Filgueira Lima and Diana Mondino linked to Mont Pelerin Society

organizations.name: Fundacion Pensar
organizations.role: Think tank of PRO party. Merged with Macri's political party. All Macri administration officials were on its board; Macri was honorary president.
organizations.atlas_relationship: Former Atlas partner (no longer listed on Atlas website as of recent checks)
organizations.political_significance: Direct conduit between Atlas ideology and Argentine state power during Macri presidency (2015-2019)

organizations.name: Asociacion Argentina de Contribuyentes (AAC)
organizations.role: Tax reform advocacy. Won Atlas Network 2024 Latin America Liberty Award ($270,000 prize pool).
organizations.atlas_relationship: Direct partner, received grants and skills-based training
organizations.achievement: Eliminated monthly credit card tax — $300 million annual savings for 3 million people. Milei endorsed their work.

organizations.name: Fundacion Federalismo y Libertad
organizations.role: Provincial think tank. Member of Atlas Network, RELIAL, and Friedrich Naumann Foundation.
organizations.recognition: Cataloged among top 100 Think Tanks worldwide in 'Global Go To Think Tank Index' since 2015

organizations.name: Fundacion Bases
organizations.role: Libertarian think tank associated with Atlas Network

organizations.name: CADAL (Centro para la Apertura y el Desarrollo de America Latina)
organizations.role: Human rights and democratic solidarity. Also receives US State Department funding (Bureau of Democracy, Human Rights and Labor).
organizations.dual_funding: US State Department DRL + Atlas Network

organizations.name: Instituto Acton Argentina
organizations.role: Promotes free market economics in harmony with Catholic Faith. Cooperates with US-based Acton Institute (Michigan).
organizations.atlas_relationship: Partner. Chafuen moved from Atlas to Acton Institute after stepping down as Atlas CEO.
organizations.programs: Poverty University — connecting economic opportunity with human dignity

organizations.name: Ayn Rand Center Argentina
organizations.role: Atlas Network affiliate focused on Objectivist philosophy

stage_4_political_capture.description: How Atlas-funded think tanks connect to Milei's government and Argentine politics

milei_connections.atlas_connection: Director of Atlas Network-sponsored think tank. Cato Institute adjunct scholar.
Member of Academic Council of Fundacion Atlas 1853. Mont Pelerin Society connection.
Former Atlas CEO (1991-2018), founding Donors Trust member, now at Acton Institute

stage_4_political_capture.milei_atlas_activity: Milei moved between Atlas institutes using them as hubs for disseminating his ideas, attending conferences and events at Fundacion Libertad y Progreso, Federalismo y Libertad, and Fundacion Atlas
stage_4_political_capture.atlas_own_assessment: Atlas Network published 'Sharpening Milei's Chainsaw: Atlas Network Partners Spur Reform' — openly claiming credit for Milei's reform agenda
stage_4_political_capture.macri_precedent: Fundacion Pensar (Atlas partner) merged directly with Macri's PRO party. All Macri officials sat on its board.

legal_bypass_mechanism.prohibition: Argentina's Ley 26.215 (Ley de Financiamiento de los Partidos Politicos) explicitly prohibits political parties from receiving contributions from foreign governments or public entities. All contributions must be traceable via bank transfer, deposit, or electronic means.
legal_bypass_mechanism.sanctions: Violations punishable by fines equal to 1x-10x the contribution amount

bypass_method.description: The pipeline does not technically violate Ley 26.215 because funds flow to NGOs/think tanks registered as civil associations (fundaciones/asociaciones civiles), NOT directly to political parties. These organizations conduct 'economic education,' 'policy research,' and 'training' — activities legally distinct from campaign contributions.
steps: 1. Koch foundations and dark money vehicles (Donors Trust) donate to Atlas Network in the US — legal under US tax law as 501(c)(3) charitable giving
2. Atlas Network grants funds to Argentine think tanks for 'economic education' projects — legal as international philanthropic grants to NGOs
3. Argentine think tanks train cadres, produce policy papers, host events, build media presence, and create the ideological ecosystem that produces political candidates
4. Think tank personnel move directly into government (Diana Mondino from Fundacion Atlas to Foreign Ministry; Fundacion Pensar board became Macri's cabinet)
5. No Argentine campaign finance law is technically violated because no direct party contribution occurs
additional_channels: NED/CIPE (US government money) — $1M to Fundacion Libertad 1985-1987 as seed funding
Corporate sponsorship from Argentine companies (Vicentin, Paladini, Nova) — domestic, legal
US State Department Bureau of Democracy funds to CADAL — legal government-to-NGO grants
Friedrich Naumann Foundation (German liberal party foundation) to Federalismo y Libertad
bypass_method.key_loophole: Argentine law has no restrictions on NGO control by foreign entities or grantors, and no restrictions on lobbying activities by NGOs. A charity can be controlled, even indirectly, by a for-profit entity or by a foreign grantor.

koch_network_scale.description: Total Koch political spending for context on the scale of the operation
koch_network_scale.note: The $12M to Latin America is a tiny fraction of Koch network spending but goes very far in Argentine political economics. For context, the AAC tax reform that Milei endorsed cost $300M/year in foregone tax revenue, achieved with Atlas grants in the low six figures.

milei_international_network.description: Milei's connections to the broader US right-wing ecosystem beyond Atlas

connections.relationship: Milei spoke at CPAC 2024, met Trump backstage. Hosted CPAC Argentina in Buenos Aires (Dec 4, 2024). Invited to speak at 2025 CPAC Circle Retreat.
connections.lobbyist: Damian Merlo (Latin America Advisory Group) — connected Milei to Heritage Foundation. Same lobbyist for Nayib Bukele.

connections.relationship: Heritage VP Derrick Morgan gave Milei a copy of 'Mandate for Leadership' (Project 2025 blueprint) at CPAC. Heritage has published multiple articles praising 'Milei Miracle.'

Visited Milei at Casa Rosada 3 times in 2024. Met with Marcos Galperin (Argentina's richest man). Thiel said Milei's Argentina 'could be the future of Europe and the United States.' Palantir connection raises data sovereignty concerns.
connections.ideological_alignment: Both believe 'freedom and democracy are not compatible'

connections.relationship: Milei's 30% spending cuts became model for Musk's DOGE. Musk attended free-market event via Zoom to toast Milei. Milei gifted Musk a 'bureaucracy chainsaw.' Musk praised Milei for 'deleting entire departments.'
14 Milei trips to US. First South American leader with formal Oval Office bilateral. $20B IMF/Treasury lifeline. Trade framework agreement. Milei member of Trump's Board of Peace.
Argentine economists Diana Mondino and Eduardo Filgueira Lima connected to Mont Pelerin via Libertad y Progreso. Milei's ideology traces directly to Hayek and the MPS tradition.
Alberto Benegas Lynch Jr. (Milei's intellectual mentor) is Cato adjunct scholar. Libertad y Progreso received Atlas funding in Jan 2024 to promote reforms jointly with Cato.

us_government_funding_channels.description: In addition to private Koch/Atlas money, US government funds also reach Argentine think tanks

channels.mechanism: Grants through CIPE (Center for International Private Enterprise, affiliated with US Chamber of Commerce)
channels.note: Atlas managed to turn US taxpayer money from NED/CIPE into an important funding source for its growing network

channels.source: US State Department — Bureau of Democracy, Human Rights and Labor (DRL)

channels.mechanism: German liberal party foundation — official partner of Federalismo y Libertad
channels.note: Extends the pipeline beyond US funding to European liberal party networks

key_findings: The Koch→Atlas→Argentine think tank pipeline has operated since at least 1985 (NED/CIPE seed funding to Fundacion Libertad)
Alejandro Chafuen (Argentine-born) led Atlas for 27 years (1991-2018) and was simultaneously a founding member of Donors Trust, the 'dark money ATM of the right'
At least 12 Argentine organizations are Atlas affiliates, receiving funding, training, networking, and competitive grants
Atlas Network itself openly claims credit for shaping Milei's reform agenda ('Sharpening Milei's Chainsaw')

The pipeline legally bypasses Argentina's ban on foreign political donations by routing money through NGOs doing 'economic education' — Argentine law places no restrictions on foreign control of NGOs or on NGO lobbying
Think tank personnel move directly into government: Fundacion Pensar board became Macri's cabinet; Diana Mondino went from Fundacion Atlas Academic Council to Milei's Foreign Ministry
The $12M flowing to Latin America 2010-2021 is a tiny fraction of Koch network spending ($1.1B in 2020 cycle alone) but achieves outsized political impact in Argentina's economy
Milei's network extends beyond Atlas to CPAC, Heritage Foundation, Peter Thiel (3 visits to Casa Rosada), Elon Musk (DOGE model), and Trump ($20B lifeline)
The same lobbyist (Damian Merlo) who connected Milei to Heritage Foundation also represents Bukele — suggesting coordinated US right-wing engagement with Latin American allies
Atlas Network does not publicly disclose its member organizations as of October 2024, making full transparency impossible

relationships: FUNDS: Atlas Network → each Argentine think tank
FUNDS: ExxonMobil → Atlas Network → Fundacion Libertad
FUNDS: US State Dept DRL → CADAL
FUNDS: Friedrich Naumann → Federalismo y Libertad
MEMBER_OF: Mondino → Fundacion Atlas Academic Council
VISITED: Peter Thiel → Casa Rosada (3x in 2024)
MODELED: Milei spending cuts → Musk DOGE
